description: NSAIDs increase risk of GI bleeding when combined with anticoagulants.
recommendation: Avoid combination. Use paracetamol for pain relief instead.
Monitor INR closely during and after antibiotic course.

description: NSAIDs reduce efficacy of ACE inhibitors and increase kidney risk.
recommendation: Use paracetamol instead. If NSAID necessary, monitor kidney function.

description: Alcohol increases risk of lactic acidosis with metformin.
recommendation: Avoid alcohol or limit to occasional small amounts.

description: Grapefruit increases statin levels, raising risk of side effects.
recommendation: Avoid grapefruit juice or limit to small amounts.

description: Calcium supplements reduce absorption of levothyroxine.
recommendation: Take levothyroxine at least 4 hours apart from calcium.

description: Iron supplements reduce absorption of levothyroxine.
recommendation: Take levothyroxine at least 4 hours apart from iron.

description: Antacids significantly reduce absorption of ciprofloxacin.
recommendation: Take ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacids.

description: Alcohol enhances sedative effects of antihistamines.
ACE inhibitors increase potassium levels; supplements may cause hyperkalemia.
recommendation: Monitor potassium levels. Avoid supplements unless prescribed.

description: PPIs reduce activation of clopidogrel, reducing its efficacy.
recommendation: Use pantoprazole instead if PPI needed with clopidogrel.

description: Ibuprofen may reduce cardioprotective effect of low-dose aspirin.
recommendation: Take aspirin 30 minutes before ibuprofen if both needed.

description: Contrast dye with metformin increases risk of lactic acidosis.
recommendation: Stop metformin 48 hours before and after contrast procedures.

description: Combined use may cause fatal cardiac arrhythmias.
